attributes.action: Give Voters a Say When Election Promises Are Broken
attributes.additional_details: We call on the Government to give voters the right to vote on whether a General Election should be held if an independent body determines that more than 33% of a governing party’s manifesto promises have been broken without exceptional justification. This would improve accountability, encourage honest campaigning, and help restore public trust in politics by giving voters a democratic voice between elections.
attributes.background: We call on the Government to give voters the right to vote on whether a General Election should be held if a significant number of election promises are broken. This would increase accountability, encourage honesty in campaigns, and help restore trust in politics.

rejection.details:
We think this petition is very similar to yours: 

Give people a vote of confidence in their MP 18 months after a general election: https://petition.parliament.uk/petitions/758722
